title: STOP MADURO Act
plain_summary: This bill allows the US government to pay up to 100 million dollars as a reward for information that helps catch and convict Nicolas Maduro. Maduro is the leader of Venezuela who has been charged with serious crimes like drug trafficking and terrorism. The reward money can only come from assets and property that the US has already taken away from Maduro and his officials. The goal is to encourage people to provide information that will help arrest Maduro for flooding the US with illegal drugs like cocaine.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Foreign Affairs.

sections.title: Section 1  Short Titles
sections.text: This law can be called the Securing Timely Opportunities for Payment and Maximizing Awards for Detaining Unlawful Regime Officials Act of 2025 or the STOP MADURO Act.

sections.title: Section 2  Findings
sections.text: Congress has found the following facts. In 2020, the Trump administration filed criminal charges against Nicolas Maduro Moros, who leads the government of Venezuela. They also charged other officials who work with him. The charges included taking part in a drug terrorism conspiracy, which means working with terrorists to sell illegal drugs. This crime has a required minimum prison sentence of twenty years and could result in life in prison. They also charged him with planning to bring cocaine into the United States, which has a required minimum sentence of ten years and could result in life in prison. The charges also included using and carrying machine guns and dangerous weapons while committing these drug crimes. This charge has a required minimum sentence of thirty years and could result in life in prison. Finally, they charged him with planning to use and carry these weapons during the drug crimes, which could result in life in prison. On March twenty sixth, 2020, United States Attorney Geoffrey S. Berman from the Southern District of New York made a public statement. He said that they announced criminal charges against Nicolas Maduro for running a drug terrorism partnership with a group called FARC for the past twenty years. He said Maduro worked with his top assistants on this. Berman explained that the large amount of drug trafficking was only possible because Maduro and others corrupted Venezuela's government institutions. They provided political and military protection for widespread drug terrorism crimes. According to the charges, Maduro and the other defendants clearly intended to flood the United States with cocaine to harm the health and wellbeing of our nation. Maduro deliberately used cocaine as a weapon. The press release also included a statement from United States Attorney Ariana Fajardo Orshan from the Southern District of Florida. She said that in recent years, her office and federal law enforcement partners worked together to bring dozens of criminal charges against high level government officials and their partners in crime. This resulted in seizing approximately four hundred fifty million dollars.

sections.title: Section 3  Increasing the Maximum Reward Amount for Information Leading to the Arrest and Conviction of Nicolas Maduro Moros Through the Department of State Rewards Program
sections.text: The Secretary of State is allowed to pay up to one hundred million dollars as a reward. This reward goes to one or more people who provide information that directly leads to the arrest and conviction of Nicolas Maduro Moros. This is allowed even though existing law normally limits how much the State Department can pay for rewards. The money for this reward must come only from selling assets that are being held away from Nicolas Maduro Moros, officials of the Maduro government, and their partners in crime. The President or the Office of Foreign Assets Control of the Department of the Treasury is holding these assets under several laws and executive orders. These include the Foreign Narcotics Kingpin Designation Act, which targets major drug traffickers. They also include section 5 of the Venezuela Defense of Human Rights and Civil Society Act of 2014. Other sources include Executive Order 13692, which blocks property and stops entry of certain people contributing to the situation in Venezuela. Executive Order 13850 blocks property of additional people contributing to the situation in Venezuela. Executive Order 13884 blocks property of the Government of Venezuela. The money can also come from assets held under any other sanctions provision.
